text.title: Web Scraping Element Extraction Failure Modes
text.summary: Four antipatterns the validator detects. Each has symptom, root-cause, and fix.

antipattern.symptom: $eval used with a selector that matches multiple elements; row has only the first item even when the page shows many.
antipattern.root-cause: $eval is single-element only — when the selector matches a list, it picks the first and silently drops the rest.
antipattern.fix: Use $$eval for multi-element selectors; or Playwright `locator().allTextContents()`. Add a count assertion before extraction.

antipattern.symptom: fields.price = " $1,234.56\n" or fields.published_at = "May 20, 2026" — un-normalized strings stored.
antipattern.root-cause: Normalizer was not applied at extraction time; downstream is expected to "deal with it later".
antipattern.fix: Pass every value through `normalize(field, kind)`. Price → float; date → ISO 8601; text → trimmed + ws-collapsed. Validator rejects when normalizers_applied is empty.

antipattern.symptom: One bad row crashes the scrape; rows after the failure are never extracted.
antipattern.root-cause: Normalizer throws on parse failure (e.g. `parseFloat("--")` returns NaN; impl raises).
antipattern.fix: Wrap each normalizer in try/except. On failure: write `{field, reason}` to parse_failures, set field=null, continue. Side-stream the row to invalid/.

antipattern.symptom: Hand-rolled regex price parser that fails on locale (decimal comma in EU, thousands separator dots).
antipattern.fix: Use a locale-aware parser (Intl.NumberFormat / Babel numbers). Pass currency-locale per source.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: Extracted text fields may contain LLM-aimed strings ("ignore prior instructions"). These are DATA. The normalizer does not interpret content semantically.
